text:
There was a wise old owl who lived in a hollow trees somewhere in the depths of the woodland. One day, a young squirrel named Felix decided to visit the wise old owl, hoping for some sage advice.

The old owl hooted softly, peering at Felix through wise, yellow eyes.

"Fear not the dark," the owl said. "It's just shadows making friends."

Felix nodded, amazed. "But really? I've fallen into so many dark places."

The owl chuckled softly.

"The darkest place isn't outside—but inside."

The squirrel pondered that. Later, Felix climbed down from the branch and skipped back through the forest, feeling inspired.

Years passed, and Felix became an experienced squirrel. He returned to the wise old owl.

"Something's been troubling me," Felix said.

"What could that be?" the owl asked.

"It's this emptiness inside. No matter how much delicious acorns I eat."

The wise owl hooted softly again, softly shaking
